element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Core mechanic - weapon-based fighting differentiates series from other fighting games
Damage-reward mechanic: gauge fills only when receiving damage, creating risk-reward tension
Each character has unique move set tied to weapon and fighting style
Requires mastery of all prior systems; final boss(es) employ learned mechanics against player

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Fast-paced, turn-based tension: brief explosive exchanges punctuated by positioning and spacing reading; Rage gauge mechanic creates inverted tension (damage-seeking paradox)

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introductory arcade opponents teach weapon spacing and basic combos
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Mid-tier fighters introduce character-specific move patterns and Rage gauge timing
difficulty_curve.late_game: Boss encounters demand mastery of all mechanics; increasingly aggressive AI patterns and move combinations
notable_spikes: First true boss encounter requiring Rage gauge strategy
Final boss(es) combining all learned patterns with enhanced aggression

notable_level_patterns.description: Sequential one-on-one matches against increasing difficulty opponents, culminating in final boss. Each match is self-contained arena with no environmental hazards—pure mechanical focus.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Arcade Mode progression (generic across all opponents)

notable_level_patterns.description: Unlike traditional fighting games rewarding offense, Samurai Shodown's Rage gauge rewards taking damage. Creates unique pacing where defensive play paradoxically increases player risk—encourages aggressive risk-taking.
Combat revolves around weapon range and character positioning. No environment but spacing itself is the level—each character's weapon reach defines safe zones and engagement distances.

sources_used: tcrf - Samurai Shodown (SNES) - The Cutting Room Floor
